arts.ttl: Nuno proud of players as Forest end wait for Anfield win against Liverpool
arts.sum: Liverpool 0-1 Nottingham Forest Nottingham Forest beat Liverpool at Anfield for the first time since 1969, as Nuno Espírito Santo’s side continued their impressive early form the new Premier League campaign. A goal by Callum Hudson-Odoi in the 72nd minute ended Liverpool’s 100% record and ensured Forest remain unbeaten under Nuno, moving up to 5th place in the English top flight in what has been an impressive start by the Reds. After four matches in the league, Forest have won two and drawn two, taking eight points and conceding just two goals. The stats represent the type of rugged, hard-to-beat side Nuno established during his tenure with Wolverhampton Wanderers, while inflicting defeat on an impressive Liverpool under new coach Arne Slot is another element to the victory. “We are very happy, we are proud of the way we did things,” Nuno said after the match. “But it required a lot of hard work. The players very hard, I don’t know the miles and distances but there’s no other way to play in Anfield. We have to overrun them – it’s all about that. The players did well. “I’m especially happy for the fans, regarding it’s so long they didn’t celebrate in Liverpool. Enjoy it, because it was a really nice moment for us. “The gameplan? If you come here to Anfield and ignore the quality your opponent has – it’s a mistake. We didn’t, we recognised that it would always require help, always leave 2v1 defensively. You cannot leave your teammate isolated because they are so good. And the gameplan was that. Try to stay in the game, try to be organised, solid and then see if you can have a moment, break them and take your chance. “I give praise to all of them. All those that participate, those that came in and impacted the game. Everybody. Because every player is gonna be needed. It’s a long season, everyone will be needed so all the squad has to be ready.” Next up for Nuno side, who introduced Jota Silva for the final ten minutes, is a tricky visit to Brighton & Hove Albion next Sunday afternoon. By @SeanGillen9

arts.ttl: Arne Slots rejects Liverpool theory and explains real reason for Nottingham Forest loss
arts.sum: Arne Slot rejected suggestions of an international hangover for his players as Liverpool suffered a shock 1-0 defeat to Nottingham Forest

arts.ttl: Arne Slot admits Liverpool's loss to Nottingham Forest is 'big setback'
arts.sum: "There were enough things we could improve from the Man United game, that’s also what we showed them. Liverpool head coach Arne Slot bemoaned his side's lack of chance creation in Saturday's 1-0 loss at home to Nottingham Forest.

arts.ttl: Owen Hargreaves names missing signing that could win Liverpool Champions League
arts.sum: TNT Sports pundit Owen Hargreaves has been impressed by Liverpool so far under Arne Slot and he believes they could be a force to be reckoned with this season in the Champions League

arts.ttl: Fonseca: ‘Milan already thinking about Liverpool and Inter’
arts.sum: Paulo Fonseca hopes the 4-0 victory over Venezia can give Milan ‘confidence’ and recover lost...

arts.ttl: Alisson takes swipe at Nottingham Forest following shock win against Liverpool
arts.sum: A second-half goal from substitute Callum Hudson-Odoi was all that separated the two teams this weekend as Forest ran out surprise 1-0 winners at Anfield. Liverpool goalkeeper Alisson claimed his side "didn't deserve to lose" against Nottingham Forest on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Gary Neville aims jibe at Liverpool after defeat to Nottingham Forest
arts.sum: Gary Neville has let Liverpool fans know how much he enjoyed their club's defeat after seeing Manchester United win earlier in the day.

arts.ttl: Liverpool's obvious failing emerges as Arne Slot receives early Anfield reminder
arts.sum: LIVERPOOL 0-1 NOTTINGHAM FOREST: Liverpool manager Arne Slot will want an immediate response from his team as he looks to rebound from a first Premier League defeat

arts.ttl: ‘Not good enough’ – Arne Slot explains where it went wrong for Liverpool in Forest defeat
arts.sum: Arne Slot had plenty to criticise about Liverpool’s performance in their shock 1-0 defeat to Nottingham Forest in the Premier League on Saturday. The Reds came into the game off an impressive three-game winning streak to start to the season, but their manager was left frustrated after Callum Hudson-Odoi secured the visitor’s first win at […] The post ‘Not good enough’ – Arne Slot explains where it went wrong for Liverpool in Forest defeat first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Arne Slot's 'big disappointment' claim speaks volumes after first Liverpool defeat
arts.sum: Liverpool head coach Arne Slot suffered his first defeat as Kop boss on Saturday, when the Reds were beaten 1-0 at Anfield by Nottingham Forest, in a smart smash-and-grab raid from Nuno Espirito Santo's men
